page: UpCloud 64xCPU-384GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 7542 processors running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration allocates 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with 2048 GB of local SSD capacity, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-threaded execution. Single-core performance is relatively low, with stress-ng single-core CPU scores falling in the bottom 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the middle 50%. However, multi-threaded capabilities are robust, as evidenced by strong bzip3 multi-threaded decompression at 461.92 GB/sec and a strong Passmark CPU score of 65404.0. Memory performance is mixed; while large-block read and write bandwidths are average, the Passmark memory score is in the bottom 25%. Database operations are highly efficient, with Redis SET operations reaching a strong 72.18 million operations per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers high resource density, combining a large core count with substantial memory and local SSD storage on a shared CPU allocation model. This makes it a viable option for workloads requiring high parallelization rather than peak single-thread speed. It is well-suited for multi-threaded data processing, large-scale web serving, caching layers, and database deployments that benefit from the 2048 GB local SSD and strong multi-core throughput.
description: The UpCloud 64xCPU-384GB is a KVM-based general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 64 physical cores operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 384.0 GB of RAM and 2048 GB of local SSD storage. While single-core benchmarks are modest, the instance delivers strong multi-threaded performance, highlighted by a Passmark CPU score of 65404.0 and 72.18 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. The shared CPU allocation model provides a cost-effective option for highly parallelized workloads. This server is suited for multi-threaded data processing, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: UpCloud 64xCPU-384GB is a KVM-based general-purpose server featuring 64 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB RAM, and 2048 GB local SSD storage, delivering strong multi-threaded performance for database and parallel workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud 64xCPU-384GB is a KVM server with 64 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB RAM, and 2048 GB SSD, optimized for multi-threaded and database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity virtual server combining sixty-four AMD EPYC cores with substantial local SSD storage for highly parallelized database and processing workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 64 physical cores and x86_64 architecture
384.0 GB of system memory and 2048 GB of local SSD storage
Strong multi-threaded performance with a Passmark CPU score of 65404.0
High-throughput database capabilities with 72.18 million Redis SET operations per second
Suited for parallel data processing, caching layers, and database deployments
